person.name: Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld

parts.value: Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sir knight Alexander HOO (4th Crusade Knight) (1166-1241) &

Dernell "Devorgulla" CANMORE (1215-1238) Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld

Alexander was born in 1166, in Luton Hoo, County Bedfordshire, England. Alexander was an Knight Hospitaller and was killed on March 8, 1241, on the Island of Rhodes in Greece on the 4th Crusade. Dernell was born in 1215, in Haddington, East Lothian, Scotland and died in 1238 in Temple, County Midlothian, Scotland.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld於1215年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓時，她的父親Alexander II, King of Scots17歲，母親Isabella de Atholl25歲。她於1237年與Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1238年3月15日在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安坦普爾享年23歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld于1215年出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾哈丁頓时，她的父亲Alexander II, King of Scots17岁，母亲Isabella de Atholl25岁。她于1237年与Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1238年3月15日在英国苏格兰米德洛迪安坦普爾享年23岁。

nameMeanings.name: Canmore,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Worcestershire): variant of   Cranmer  .

variants.name: Cranmer
meanings: English: habitational name from Cranmer Green in Walsham le Willows (Suffolk), and perhaps also from other places similarly named, such as Cranmer Hall in Sculthorpe (Norfolk), Cranmore in Shalfleet (Isle of Wight), Cranmer Bottom in Selborne and Cranmore in Aldershot (both Hampshire), and East and West Cranmore (Somerset), all named from Old English cran ‘crane’ + mere ‘lake, pool’ or mōr ‘moor, marshy ground’. See also Cranmore , with which there may have been some confusion.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Scotland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Kinross and Clackmannanshire):
from the Middle English personal name Scotland, Scolland. Found in Normandy as well as England, it evidently denoted a Scot or someone with an association with Scotland.
ethnic name for someone from Scotland, from Middle English Scotlond ‘Scotland’. Compare England .

variants.name: England
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Dunkeld
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname from Middle Low German dunkel, a byform of dunker ‘dark, conceited, unclear’, also applied as a byname for a blind person. Compare Dunckel and Dunkle .

variants.name: Dunckel
meanings: German: variant of   Dunkel  .

variants.name: Dunkle
meanings: Americanized form of German   Dunkel  .

nameMeanings.name: Dernell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain derivation. It may be a transferred use of the surname derived from darnel, a type of grass (from Old French), or it may be a variant of Darrell , influenced by the plant name.

nameMeanings.name: "Devorgulla"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Diorbhail , earlier Diorbhorguil.

en.lifeSummary: When Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld was born in 1215, in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, her father, Alexander II, King of Scots, was 17 and her mother, Isabella de Atholl, was 25. She married Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight in 1237, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 15 March 1238, in Temple, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 23.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld est née en 1215, à Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, Alexander II, King of Scots, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Atholl, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight en 1237, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 15 mars 1238, à Temple, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 23 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld 1215 in Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alexander II, King of Scots 17 und ihre Mutter Isabella de Atholl 25. Sie hat 1237 in Schottland, United Kingdom Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 15. März 1238 im Alter von 23 Jahren in Temple, Midlothian, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld è nata il nel 1215 nel Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, sua padre, Alexander II, King of Scots, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Isabella de Atholl, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight nel 1237, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 15 marzo 1238, nel Temple, Midlothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 23 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeldが1215年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアハッドイントンで生まれた時，父Alexander II, King of Scotsは17歳，母Isabella de Athollは25歳であった。1237年にイギリススコットランドでSir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1238年3月15日にイギリススコットランドミドロスアンテンプルで23歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld(이)가 1215년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 하딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alexander II, King of Scots(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella de Atholl(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1237년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1238년 3월 15일 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 템일에서 23세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld nasceu em 1215, em Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, seu pai, Alexander II, King of Scots, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Isabella de Atholl, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight em 1237, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 15 de março de 1238, em Temple, Midlothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 23 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld родилась в 1215 г. в местности хаддингтон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Alexander II, King of Scots было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Atholl, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight в 1237 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 15 марта 1238 г. в местности темпл, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 23 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dernell "Devorgulla" Canmore, Princess of Scotland and Dunkeld nació en 1215, en Haddington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, su papá, Alexander II, King of Scots, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Isabella de Atholl, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sir Knight Alexander Hoo 4th Crusade Knight en 1237, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 15 de marzo de 1238, en Temple, Midlothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 23 años.
